english: horses were trotting together Glr.; rtá-pa (rta pa) brgya (brgya) dań (dang) lhán-du (lhan du) accompanied by a hundred men on horseback Glr.; lhan(-cig)-skyes(-pa) (lhan(-cig)-skyes(-pa)) born together with, e.g. the 'lha' (lha) or 'dre' (dre) born together with every human being Mil.; lhan-skyes (lhan skyes) nad (nad), rma (rma) a hereditary disease or defect Med. lhan-rgyás (lhan rgyas) 1. 'partner of the seal', i.e. a colleague using the same seal in official business (lhan-rgyás-kyi (lhan rgyas kyi) té-tse (te tse), or spyi-dám (spyi dam)). 2. = lhan-gćig (lhan gcig), *hleń-gyé-la (*hleng gye la) tsog (tsog) or žug* (zhug*) they have come together.

I. vb., to join, to unite, *K̆a lán-će* (*Kha lan ce*) W. to kiss, *'u lán-će* (*'u lan ce*) id. resp. II. sbst. 1. a patch, lhán-pas (lhan pas) klán-pa (klan pa) B., *hlém-pa gyág-pa* (*hlem pa gyag pa*) C., *gyúb-će* (*gyub ce*) W., འདེབས་པ་ _odebs-pa ('debs pa), རྡབ་པ་ rdáb-pa (rdab pa) Mil. to patch, mend. — 2. spot, speck, blot, place differently coloured, འོད་ཟེར་ _od-zér ('od zer) śár-ćiń (shar cing) lhán-pa (lhan pa) bźín-du (bzhin du) a sunbeam forming by reflection a bright spot Dzl.; lhan-tábs (lhan tabs) appendix, supplement, title of a medical book.
, lhabs-se (lhabs se) lhabs (lhabs) Sch.: 'to flutter to and fro, to glimmer, glisten' (?).

wide, flowing, dar-bér (dar ber) lhab-lhúb (lhab lhub) a wide silk cloak; prob. also sbst.: the moving to and fro, waving, mé-tog-gi (me tog gi) of flowers Do., *hlab-hlúb-tu sol* (*hlab hlub tu sol*) C. loosen your dress! make yourself comfortable!

boot, also shoe; mćil-lham (mcil lham) id.; rgyá-lham (rgya lham) a Chinese boot, sóg-lham (sog lham) a Mongol boot. Comp. lham-skúd (lham skud) twine, used by shoemakers Schr. — lham-kań-ćén (lham kang cen) (prob. a Chinese word) strong Chinese boots C. — lhám-mkhan (lham mkhan) shoemaker Schr. — lham-sgróg (lham sgrog) shoe-strap, latchet; string for lacing felt-leggings. — lham-mtíl (lham mtil) boot-sole. — lham-yú (lham yu) leg of a boot Cs., lham yu-ríń (lham yu ring) boots with long legs Sch. — Sch.: lham-krád (lham krad) or -skrád (-skrad) pieces of leather, used for the patching of soles; lham-góg (lham gog) worn-out boots; lham-grám (lham gram) the upper-leather, the vamp; lham sgró-gu-can (lham sgro gu can) buskins; lham yu-ćád (lham yu cad) a sort of slippers to which cotton leggings are sewed (?); rtiń-lham (rting lham) quarter-piece (of a shoe).
at once, all, every thing Sch. Cf. lhem (lhem).

1. pen, fold, inclosure for sheep C., W.; also *hlé-ra* (*hle ra*). 2. also lhés(-ma) (lhes(-ma)) braid; wicker-work; texture; also of pastry, twisted cake or bun, cracknel (W. *žim-zag* (*zhim zag*)), also lhas-dóg (lhas dog).
n. of the birth-place of the mother of Buddha, Ssk.

n. of a cousin of Buddha who, as the legends have it, was continually annoying Buddha by malicious artifices, whereby, however, the blameless character of the latter showed itself but the more conspicuously; hence proverbially used for any malicious character Cs.

, cf. lińs-pa (lings pa); lhiń-skrán (lhing skran) Sch.: a tumor filled with matter, an abscess, lhiń-rtsa (lhing rtsa) a full vein; lhiń ćád-pa (lhing cad pa) Lt., acc. to Sch.: completely separated.

part, portion of the body of an animal, = 1/2 zug (zug), lhu-tsígs bću-gnyís (lhu tsigs bcu gnyis) Sch.: the 12 chief parts (of an animal) resulting from this way of dividing it, — but elsewhere 18 such parts are mentioned; lhú-ru ysil-ba (lhu ru ysil ba) to divide, to parcel out Mil.; share in plough-land, v. sub spyod-pa (spyod pa) I, 2.
v. ldúg-pa (ldug pa) to pour Cs.

, Cs.: 'lhug-pa (lhug pa) and lhug-ma (lhug ma) prose; lhug-po (lhug po) wide, diffuse, luxurious, gos (gos) lhug-lhug-po (lhug lhug po) a very ample robe; lhug-par (lhug par) amply, copiously, plentifully; lhug-par smra-ba (lhug par smra ba) to speak diffusely, copiously, to speak in prose'. Sch. adds: 'lhugs (lhugs) successive, continuous; lhugs-tsíg (lhugs tsig) and lhug-pa (lhug pa) continuous prose'. The principal meaning, however, seems to be: uninterrupted, having no gaps; unreserved; lhúg-par (lhug par) bśád-pa (bshad pa) = sbas-gsáń-med-par (sbas gsang med par) bśád-pa (bshad pa) to explain completely, without omitting any thing, *lug táń-će* (*lug tang ce*) Ld. to give unreservedly, without limitation; hence also: liberally, plentifully; mći-ma (mci ma)
